close_reason: Extracted make_temp_path() and rename_into_place() shared helpers
description: lib.rs:145-215 has two near-identical write_secret_file functions: parent-extraction, file_name-extraction, counter-fetch, tmp_name format, write+rename, error-path cleanup are all duplicated. Only the file-creation step differs (unix uses OpenOptions+mode(0o600)+create_new, non-unix uses fs::write). Extract a shared helper that takes a 'write_to_temp: impl Fn(&Path, &[u8]) -> io::Result<()>' closure parameter, then have the two cfg-gated functions only differ in that one closure. Reduces 70 lines of duplication to ~20 lines + two closures.
title: [chore] write_secret_file unix and non-unix variants share 80% of structure — extract common helper
